frases.en: Ask me for it.
Ask him for it.
Ask her for it.
Ask them for it.
I'll ask you for it.
I'll ask you for them.
Ask her for them.
Ask me for them.

frases.es: Hay un problema con el sistema nuevo.
frases.en: There's a problem with the new system.
There are several matters to take care of.

frases.es: Había mucha gente aquí cuando llegué.
frases.en: There were a lot of people here when I arrived.

frases.es: Hubo una explosión en la fábrica anoche.
frases.en: There was an explosion in the factory last night.

frases.es: Había varios faxes aquí esta mañana.
frases.en: There were several faxes here this morning.

frases.es: Habrá una fiesta esta noche si no llueve.
frases.en: There will be a party tonight if it doesn't rain.

frases.es: Va a haber una reunión esta tarde.
frases.en: There's going to be a meeting this afternoon.

frases.es: Va a haber muchos problemas con esto.
frases.en: There are going to be a lot of problems with this.
There's been a misunderstanding.
There have been several cases.

frases.es: Habría menos casos si hubiera una vacuna.
frases.en: There would be fewer cases if there were a vaccine.
There must be a solution.

frases.es: Tiene que haber una forma de resolverlo.
frases.en: There has to be a way to solve it.

frases.es: Tiene que haber al menos tres.
frases.en: There have to be at least three.
There should be more chairs.

frases.es: Podría haber más de lo que piensas.
frases.en: There could be more than you think.

frases.es: Me dijeron el otro día que había habido problemas con el sistema nuevo.
frases.en: They told me the other day that there had been problems with the new system.

frases.es: Siempre ha habido problemas con el sistema.
frases.en: There have always been problems with the system.
There doesn't seem to be a solution.

frases.es: Habría habido más problemas si no hubiéramos visto el error a tiempo.
frases.en: There would've been more problems if we hadn't seen the mistake in time.
There used to be a lot of people here.

frases.es: No hay tantos como antes había.
frases.en: There aren't as many as there used to be.

frases.es: No tiene que haber una solución necesariamente.
frases.en: There doesn't necessarily have to be a solution.
There can't be that many people.
There may be a solution.

frases.es: Puede que haya habido un malentendido.
frases.en: There may have been a misunderstanding.

frases.es: Quiero que haya una azafata en la reunión.
frases.en: I want there to be a hostess at the meeting.
Bring me whatever there is.
There's nothing left to do.
There seem to be several versions.
There won't be a party tonight.
When will there be a meeting on this?

frases.es: ¿Va a haber otra fiesta como la última?
frases.en: Is there going to be another party like the last one?

frases.es: Jamás ha habido alguien como ella.
frases.en: There's never been anyone like her.
There have been several storms lately.

frases.es: ¿Por qué no había gente cuando llegué allí?
frases.en: Why weren't there any people when I got there?
There could have been an accident.

frases.es: Debería haber habido un guardia de seguridad.
frases.en: There should have been a security guard.

frases.es: Me trajeron todo lo que había.
frases.en: They brought me everything there was.

frases.es: No hay ni la mitad de lo que antes había.
frases.en: There isn't even half of what there used to be.
Have there been any complaints lately?

frases.es: No habría quejas si Pepe estuviera aquí.
frases.en: There wouldn't be any complaints if Pepe were here.
Shouldn't there be an index?
Have there been any calls for me?
Were there any injuries?
Are there any problems?
Will there be anyone waiting for me?
Is there any chance?
Has there been any change?
Have there been any changes?

frases.es: ¿Qué le dijo (a ella) el lobo cuando la vio?
frases.en: What did the wolf say to her when he saw her?
He asked her where she was going.
What did she say?

frases.es: Ella le dijo que iba a casa de su abuela.
frases.en: She told him she was going to her grandmother's house.
And then what did she do?

frases.es: No me acuerdo del resto del cuento.
frases.en: I don't remember the rest of the story.

frases.es: ¿Se la comió el lobo al final?
frases.en: Did the wolf eat her in the end?

frases.es: Creo que un cazador la salvó.
frases.en: I think a hunter saved her.
And what happened to the wolf?
The hunter killed him / it.

frases.es: Esa no es la versión que yo conozco.
frases.en: That's not the version I know.
What difference does it make?

frases.es: Él no habla tanto como tú.
frases.en: He doesn't talk as much as you do.
You talk too much.
Don't smoke so much.
I don't smoke as much as you do.
You smoke too many cigars.
I don't smoke as many as he does.

frases.es: Fumas tantos que no sé qué hacer.
frases.en: You smoke so many that I don't know what to do.
Can't you hear me?
Can I attend the meeting?
That's life.

frases.es: No me creo nada de lo que dices.
frases.en: I don't believe anything you say.
Don't you trust me?

frases.es: No es que no me fíe de ti...
frases.en: It's not that I don't trust you...
Then what is it?
What's the matter with you?
Don't you like me any more?
Don't you want to see me any more?
I thought we were friends.

frases.es: Creí que yo era tu mejor amigo.
frases.en: I thought I was your best friend.
Why don't you say something?

frases.es: Me gustaría saber lo que piensas.
frases.en: I'd like to know what you think.
Since when do you hate me?
What did I do to offend you?
They had a good time.
They wished us a Merry Christmas.
We wished them the same.

frases.es: Él parecía estar de buen humor.
frases.en: He seemed to be in a good mood.
How do you know?
Because he said good morning to me.
There were some problems at first.
How did you solve them?
In the usual way.
How was that?
I'll tell you later.
Why can't you tell me now?
Okay, I'll tell you.
Thank you for telling me the truth.
You're welcome / Don't mention it.
Explain it to me one more time.

frases.es: Fue mucho más fácil que la última vez.
frases.en: It was much easier than the last time.
How long did it take you to do it?

frases.es: Él estaba mejor preparado que yo.
frases.en: He was better prepared than I was.
It was due to an error / a mistake.

frases.es: Él tuvo que subirse al árbol.
frases.en: He had to climb the tree.
The plumber came to look at it.

frases.es: Se estaban quejando de la falta de un plan concreto.
frases.en: They were complaining about the lack of a specific plan.
But they saw this, didn't they?

frases.es: ¿Quién estaba con él cuando se cayó?
frases.en: Who was with him when he fell?

frases.es: Todo el mundo esperaba un hombre.
frases.en: Everyone expected a man.
There will be several meetings.
Will there be many people there?

frases.es: No necesitamos tu ayuda por ahora.
frases.en: We don't need your help for now.

frases.es: Gracias a Dios no me llamaron.

frases.es: No habrá ninguna clase de fiesta.
frases.en: There won't be any kind of party.
Don't send that to me any more.
There are still some mistakes.

I can see myself in the mirror.

frases.en: What did the wolf say to her when he saw her?

frases.es: Ella le dijo que iba a casa de su abuela.
frases.en: She told him she was going to her grandmother's house.

frases.es: Él no habla tanto como tú.
frases.en: He doesn't talk as much as you do.
You talk too much.
Don't smoke so much.
I don't smoke as much as you do.
You smoke too many cigars.
I don't smoke as much as him.

frases.es: Fumo tantos que no sé qué hacer.
frases.en: You smoke so many that I don't know what to do.
Can't you hear me?
Can I attend the meeting?
That's life.

frases.es: No me creo nada de lo que dices.
frases.en: I don't believe anything you say.
Don't you trust me?

frases.es: No es que no me fie de ti...
frases.en: It's not that I don't trust you...
I'm disappointed with your progress.

frases.es: Eres capaz de hacer mayores progresos.
frases.en: You're capable of making greater progress.

frases.es: No has hecho un gran esfuerzo.
frases.en: You haven't made a big effort.
You've wasted a lot of time.
Your mother is very upset.
You should've studied much more.

frases.es: Deberías haber asistido a más clases.
frases.en: You should've attended more classes.
I have reasons to be angry.
Now what am I going to do with you?

frases.es: ¿Qué va a pensar tu abuela cuando se entere?
frases.en: What is your grandmother going to think when she finds out?
I'm almost afraid to tell her.
Do you know how much this course has cost?
I've had to make sacrifices.

frases.es: La vida no es un lecho de rosas.
frases.en: Life is not a bed of roses.

frases.es: Tienes que luchar por todo lo que consigues.
frases.en: You have to fight for everything you get.

frases.es: Nunca llegarás a ninguna parte con esa actitud.
frases.en: You'll never get anywhere with that attitude.

frases.es: No entiendo a los jóvenes de hoy en día.
frases.en: I don't understand young people nowadays.

frases.es: Cuando yo tenía tu edad, estábamos orgullosos de ir a la universidad.
frases.en: When I was your age, we were proud to go to university.
What's the matter with you?
What are you going to do now?

frases.es: ¿Crees que voy a mantenerte para siempre?
frases.en: Do you think I'm going to support you forever?

frases.es: Más vale que busques un trabajo.
frases.en: You'd better look for a job.

frases.es: Ya te hemos dado dos oportunidades.
frases.en: We've already given you two chances.
You haven't taken advantage of them.
You should be ashamed.
I've been here since this morning.

frases.es: Se enfadará si se lo dices.
frases.en: He'll get angry if you tell him.
I don't intend to tell him.

frases.es: Pero tarde o temprano se enterará.
frases.en: But he'll find out sooner or later.

frases.es: No estaré aquí cuando se entere.
frases.en: I won't be here when he finds out.

frases.es: Puede que todavía haya una solución.
frases.en: There still may be a solution.
What is it?
We could rob a bank.
That would be too dangerous.
Besides, someone could get hurt.
Then, what do you suggest we do?

frases.es: Sugiero que pidamos prestado el dinero.
frases.en: I suggest we borrow the money.
How are you going to get so much money?
There has to be a way.
Someone's been here.
It's been raining for three days.

frases.es: ¿Por qué no han hecho nada todavía?
frases.en: Why haven't they done anything yet?

frases.es: No se les dijo que hicieran nada.
frases.en: They weren't told to do anything.
The war's over.
I'm tired of waiting for him.
How long does it usually take him to get there?
It usually takes him about an hour.
I wonder where he is.
Something must have happened to him.

frases.es: Es extraño que no haya llegado.
frases.en: It's strange that he hasn't arrived.

frases.es: A lo mejor ha tenido un accidente.
frases.en: Maybe he's had an accident.
How long are you going to wait?
A few more minutes.
And then what will you do?
I have no idea.
There have been some changes recently.

frases.es: ¿De qué clase de cambios estás hablando?
frases.en: What kind of changes are you talking about?

frases.es: Te van a trasladar a mi departamento.
frases.en: They're going to transfer you to my department.

frases.es: ¿Quieres decir que vamos a trabajar juntos?
frases.en: Do you mean we're going to work together?
That's right.
It's about time.
Where did you get that information?

frases.es: Lo sé desde hace mucho tiempo.
frases.en: I've known about it for a long time.
Why didn't you say anything to me?
I promised to keep it a secret.

frases.es: Pero podías habérmelo dicho a mí.
frases.en: But you could have told me.

frases.es: Pero la decisión no era definitiva.
frases.en: But the decision wasn't final.
Is it now?

frases.es: Todo el mundo será informado mañana.
frases.en: Everyone will be informed tomorrow.
Will it mean a raise / rise?
Of course it will.
How long have you been planning this?

frases.es: Ha sido idea mía desde hace mucho tiempo.
frases.en: It's been an idea of mine for a long time.
Who will I report to?
You'll be my right-hand man.
You should have told me.

frases.es: No tenía ni idea de que estabas enferma.
frases.en: I had no idea you were ill.

frases.es: Me enteré de ello a través de Juana.
frases.en: I learned about it through Jane.

frases.es: Menos mal que tropecé con ella.
frases.en: It's a good thing I ran into her.
What did the doctors tell you?
How did that happen to you?
When will you leave the hospital?

frases.es: Si pudiera pagarlo, te llevaría a la Clínica Pérez.
frases.en: If I could pay for it, I'd take you to the Pérez Clinic.

frases.es: Mantenme al día de cómo vas.
frases.en: Keep me up to date on how you're doing.
What a surprise!
You look good.
Let's change the subject.
Let's not talk about illnesses.
They depress me.
I get depressed easily.
Come on, cheer up!
Talk to me about verbs.
Why do you want to talk about verbs?
Aren't you sick of practising verbs?
It depends on what verbs we practise.
Haven't we covered all the verbs?
We haven't seen the conditional.
But the conditional is simple.
Maybe it is for you.
And for you too.

frases.es: Me lío con ese tiempo verbal.
frases.en: I get mixed up with that verb tense.
Okay, if you insist.
You look tired.

frases.es: ¿Y tú no lo estarías después de lo que he hecho yo?
frases.en: And wouldn't you after what I've done?
What have you done?

frases.es: Acabo de terminar de escribir más de 4.000 frases.
frases.en: I've just finished writing more than 4,000 sentences.
What do you mean?

frases.es: Quiero decir que todo hasta ahora ha sido fácil para mí.
frases.en: I mean that everything up to now has been easy for me.
You think you're really smart.

frases.es: Fallé muy pocos en el último libro.
frases.en: I missed very few in the last book.
Are you looking for a challenge?
I want something to give me a push.
I want something challenging.

frases.es: Quiero que me pongas a prueba.
frases.en: I want you to test me.
I want you to make me sweat.

frases.es: Quiero el inglés como tú lo hablas.
frases.en: I want English like you speak it.

frases.es: Pero los seis primeros libros contienen el inglés de uso corriente.
frases.en: But the first six books contain normal English.

frases.es: Entonces, ¿por qué me cuesta tanto entenderte cuando hablas?
frases.en: Then why do I have so much trouble understanding you when you speak?

frases.es: A lo mejor deberías limpiarte los oídos.
frases.en: Maybe you should clean out your ears.

frases.es: ¿Ves? Acabas de usar un verbo compuesto.
frases.en: You see? You've just used a phrasal verb.
There are thousands of those verbs.
Teach them to me.

frases.es: Pero son imposibles de asimilar si no vives en el país.
frases.en: But they're impossible to assimilate if you don't live in the country.
I accept the challenge.

frases.es: Vale, tú te lo has buscado.
frases.en: Okay, you're asking for it.

frases.es: Si yo supiera su nombre, le llamaría.
frases.en: If I knew his name, I'd call him.
temas_gramaticales: Possession: possessive adjectives: my, your, his, her, our, their

frases.en: Look it up in the telephone book.

frases.es: Podría hacerlo si tuviera una guía telefónica.
frases.en: I could do it if I had a telephone book.
Your neighbour probably has one.

frases.en: Are you sure?
Would you quit bothering me?
Do you want me to leave?

frases.es: Quiero que te calles, eso es todo.
frases.en: I want you to shut up, that's all.

frases.es: ¿Qué harías si yo desapareciera de la faz de la tierra?
frases.en: What would you do if I disappeared from the face of the earth?
I'd jump for joy.
Thanks a lot!
What a friend!
I'm pulling your leg.

frases.en: Since when have you been a joker?
I've always been one.

frases.es: Esa es una faceta de tu carácter que nunca he visto.
frases.en: That's a facet of your character I've never seen.
Do you think I'm a serious person?
You're a very intense person.
You don't know me.

frases.es: Habla con mi mujer, ella te lo dirá.
frases.en: Talk to my wife; she'll tell you.
Okay, if you say so.
Now then, where were we?
We were with the conditional.

frases.es: ¡Ah, sí! ¿Y qué harías si yo te apuntara con una pistola?
frases.en: That's right! Now, what would you do if I pointed a gun at you?
I'd start shaking.

frases.es: Se me pusieron los pelos de punta.
frases.en: My hair stood on end.
I was scared to death.
I started shaking.
The teacher calmed me down.

frases.es: El miedo es una sensación aterradora.
frases.en: Fear is a terrifying sensation.

frases.es: Se me fue el santo al cielo.
frases.en: It completely slipped my mind.

frases.es: Deja de meterte el dedo en la nariz, ¿quieres?
frases.en: Stop picking your nose, will you?

frases.es: Está tan lejos que apenas lo puedo divisar.
frases.en: It's so far away that I can hardly make it out.
It should hardly ever be done.

frases.es: Ese tipo es un pesado de narices.
frases.en: That guy is a pain in the neck.
What a drag it is getting up!

frases.es: Ella tiene un don para los idiomas.
frases.en: She has a gift for languages.
She's a gifted child.

frases.es: Tiene un coeficiente de inteligencia muy alto.
frases.en: She has a very high I.Q.

frases.es: Me estoy liando con tantos datos.
frases.en: I'm getting mixed up with so much data.
You're getting me mixed up.

frases.es: ¿Qué tiene que ver esto con aquello?
frases.en: What does this have to do with that?

frases.es: Por mucho que trabajes no vas a ganar más dinero.
frases.en: No matter how hard you work, you're not going to earn more money.
No matter how intelligent you are...
No matter how often you go...
No matter how fast you drive...
No matter how hard you pressure him...
No matter how many vitamins you take...
Call it off.
Why do you put up with that behaviour?

frases.es: Dile que se ocupe de sus propios asuntos.
frases.en: Tell him to mind his own business.

frases.en: He's prying into your private life.

frases.en: Get away from the steam.
I'm going to turn up the heat on them.

frases.es: Voy a hacerles sudar un poco.
frases.en: I'm going to make them sweat a little.

frases.es: No te asomes por la ventana.
frases.en: Don't lean out the window.

frases.es: No te apoyes en la pared.
frases.en: Don't lean against the wall.

frases.es: Estamos respirando el humo de ese tubo de escape.
frases.en: We're breathing the fumes from that exhaust pipe.

frases.es: No puedo evitar reírme delante de él.
frases.en: I can't help laughing in front of him.
He has such a big nose!

frases.es: Hay algo en él que atrae a las mujeres.
frases.en: There's something about him that attracts women.

frases.es: Quizá sea la forma en que sonríe.
frases.en: Maybe it's the way he smiles.

frases.es: Tienes que reconocer que no es guapo.
frases.en: You have to admit he's not handsome.
He's only ugly on the outside.

frases.es: Por dentro es una persona maravillosa.
frases.en: On the inside he's a wonderful person.

frases.es: Me casaría con él si me lo pidiera.
frases.en: I'd marry him if he asked me to.

frases.es: Tiene un don que pocos hombres poseen.
frases.en: He has a gift that few men possess.

frases.es: Se siente su presencia cuando entra por la puerta.

frases.en: I almost faint whenever he looks at me.

frases.en: It's too bad he's married.

frases.en: The renewal date has been changed.

frases.es: Te estás saliendo por la tangente.
frases.en: You're going off on a tangent.

frases.es: Se habría publicado antes si no se hubiera inundado el almacén.

Call me as soon as you know something.

frases.es: Lo siento... lo hice sin querer.
frases.en: I'm sorry... I didn't mean to do it.

frases.es: ¿Te das cuenta de lo que me has hecho?
frases.en: Do you realize what you've done to me?

frases.es: ¿Cómo voy a salir de este lío?
frases.en: How am I going to get out of this mess?

frases.es: Si no hubieran quebrado, podríamos haber cobrado la cantidad.
frases.en: If they hadn't gone bankrupt, we could've collected the amount.
It's too late now.
What shall we do now?

frases.es: No nos digas lo que debíamos hacer.
frases.en: Don't tell us what we should've done.

frases.es: Dinos lo que deberíamos hacer ahora.

frases.es: Tenemos suficiente para llegar a mayo.
frases.en: We have enough to last us until May.

frases.en: And if we lengthen the terms of payment?

frases.es: Son en su mayoría pequeños proveedores.
